title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Protocol Clinic: Net Interface Counters
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net interface counters increment in an evidence-backed incident resolution packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Interface Counters evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-interface-counters-9jlo4i" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Interface Counters into observable performance evidence.
